REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF OKSANA CLEM MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE LEASE AND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO STATE ANY LEGAL GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION AS REQUIRED BY THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. POINTS AND AUTHORITIES ARE DEVOID OF ANY AUTHORITIES ADDRESSING THE RELIEF REQUESTED. IT IS NOT CLEAR WHAT MOTION THE PLAINTIFF IS ATTEMPTING TO BRING, BUT NONE OF THE MOTIONS THAT COURT COULD CONSTRUE THIS AS WOULD BE PROCEDURALLY OR SUBSTANTIVELY PROPER, E.G. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, ETC. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE FORMATTING REQUIREMENTS OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, E.G. THE BRIEFS ARE SINGLE-SPACED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE CONTRACT DENIED. CALIFORNIA LAW DOES NOT RECOGNIZE A MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE CONTRACT. EVEN IF THE MOTION IS CHARACTERIZED AS A ONE FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, IT FAILS DUE TO INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE AND NON-COMPLIANCE WITH PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR THESE MOTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2024 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Defendant'S Requests For
Admissions Admitted Against Plaintiff, And Request For
Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jun-21-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT 1140 HARRISON ASSOCIATES LP, MCLAUGHLIN SANCHEZ LLP's Motion To Deem Defendant'S Requests For Admissions Admitted Against Plaintiff, And Request For
Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court acknowledges the Dismissal of Entire Action filed on Jun-20-2024. The court's tentative ruling is withdrawn and the subject motion is taken off calendar.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-18-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE CONTRACT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE CONTRACT Denied. California law does not recognize a motion to remove collections and invalidate contract. Even if the motion is characterized as a one for summary judgment or injunctive relief, it fails due to insufficient evidence and non-compliance with procedural requirements for these motions.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion To Remove Collections And Invalidate Contract
Off Calendar Apr-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF OKSANA CLEM's Motion To Remove Collections And Invalidate Contract.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Transferred to be heard in Department 501 at 9:30 a.m. on the same day. That department handles all real property cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Motion To Remove Collections And Invalidate Lease And Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Oksana Clem, Plaintiff in Pro per via zoom
Michael J. McLaughlin, Esq., McLauglin Sanchez LLP, 415-665-9753, via zoom for defendants

Ms. Clem contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF OKSANA CLEM MOTION TO REMOVE COLLECTIONS AND INVALIDATE LEASE AND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT

is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to state any legal grounds for the motion in the Notice of Motion as required by the California Rules of Court. Points and Authorities are devoid of any authorities addressing the relief requested. It is not clear what motion the Plaintiff is attempting to bring, but none of the motions that Court could construe this as would be procedurally or substantively proper, e.g. Motion for Summary Judgment, Preliminary Injunction, Judgment on the Pleadings, etc. The Court further notes that the moving party failed to comply with the formatting requirements of the California Rules of Court, e.g. the briefs are single-spaced.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
